p.seg: To all Holy Mother Church's sons, Robert de Quincy [sends] greeting.

Let present and future know me to have given, conceded, and by this my charter made firm, to God, the church of the Holyrood at Edinburgh, and to the canons serving God in that place, one ploughgate of land and ten acres in the territory of Tranent, in the direction of Fa'side, for the exchange of that ploughgate of land at Preston which the canons aforesaid claimed against me and the monks of Newbattle, so that the aforenamed canons quitclaimed me and my heirs and the aforesaid monks over that claim of the aforesaid land of Preston in return for the aforesaid exchange. That is why I, Robert de Quincy, gave, conceded, and by this my charter made firm, to the aforesaid canons the aforenamed land with ten acres of land, to be held in pure and everlasting alms of me and my heirs, with my men of Tranent's common pasture pertaining to so much land, and with free right of egress towards the aforesaid common pasture; as freely, quitly, and honourably as any alms is most freely, quitly, honourably held and possessed in the kingdom of the king of Scotland.
